nonGoals: Do NOT modify any TypeScript code, handler logic, or seed data
Do NOT change any existing schema fields or remove anything
objective: Add Capabilities schema object and SynthesisJobStatus enum to the OpenAPI specification, and extend Provider and Voice component schemas with an optional `capabilities` field.
planText: Open `openapi.yaml`. 1) Add a `SynthesisJobStatus` enum schema under `components/schemas` with values: `pending`, `running`, `completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`. 2) Add a `Capabilities` object schema with properties: `maxTextLength` (integer, nullable), `supportsInstructions` (boolean), `supportsStreaming` (boolean), `outputFormats` (array of strings). 3) Extend the `Provider` component schema by adding an optional `capabilities` property referencing `$ref: '#/components/schemas/Capabilities'`. 4) Extend the `Voice` component schema by adding an optional `capabilities` property referencing `$ref: '#/components/schemas/Capabilities'` (with a description noting: inherits from provider unless overridden at voice level). Validate the spec with a YAML/OpenAPI linter if available.

nonGoals: Do NOT change any existing type fields or remove anything
Do NOT populate runtime data — only type declarations
Do NOT modify any endpoint handlers or serialization logic
objective: Add `capabilities` optional property to the Provider and Voice TypeScript type definitions used across the codebase.
planText: 1) Define a `Capabilities` interface: `{ maxTextLength: number | null; supportsInstructions: boolean; supportsStreaming: boolean; outputFormats: string[]; }`. 2) Define a `SynthesisJobStatus` enum or string union: `'pending' | 'running' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'cancelled'`. 3) Add an optional `capabilities?: Capabilities` field to the existing `Provider` type/interface. 4) Add an optional `capabilities?: Capabilities` field to the existing `Voice` type/interface. 5) Ensure the types are exported and importable by endpoint handlers and test files. Run `npm run test` to confirm no type errors.

nonGoals: Do NOT change endpoint handlers or serialization
Do NOT alter existing provider fields like id, name, or sampleRate
objective: Populate `capabilities` data for every active provider in the seed/static provider data source.
planText: Open the provider seed data file (likely `apps/web/src/lib/data/providers.ts` or the static JSON source). For each provider record, add a `capabilities` block matching the Capabilities type: `maxTextLength` (provider-appropriate limit or null), `supportsInstructions` (true for SSML-capable providers), `supportsStreaming` (true for streaming-capable providers), `outputFormats` (array of supported output MIME types like `['audio/mpeg']`, `['audio/wav']`, etc.). At minimum cover `openai` and every other active provider. If voice-level overrides are needed, annotate specific voice records in the voices data file with their own `capabilities` block only where they differ from the provider default. Run `npm run test`.

nonGoals: Do NOT change the response shape beyond adding `capabilities`
Do NOT modify voice endpoint logic
Do NOT add new query parameters or filtering
objective: Update the `/providers.json` SvelteKit endpoint handler to serialize the `capabilities` field in each provider response object.
planText: Locate the `/providers.json` route handler in `apps/web/src/routes/providers.json/+server.ts`. Ensure the handler reads provider data that now includes the `capabilities` field (from deliverable 2) and that the JSON serialization passes it through unchanged. If the handler currently picks/excludes fields explicitly, add `capabilities` to the inclusion list. No inheritance logic is needed at the provider level. Run `npm run test` to verify.

nonGoals: Do NOT change the Voice type or seed data (already done in deliverables 1-2)
Do NOT modify the provider endpoint
Do NOT break existing voice fields
objective: Update the `/api/v1/voices.json` SvelteKit endpoint handler to serialize `capabilities` per voice, applying the inheritance rule: voice inherits its provider's capabilities unless the voice has its own explicit `capabilities` override.
planText: Open the voices.json route handler. For each voice, look up its parent provider's `capabilities`. If the voice record has its own non-null `capabilities` field, use it as-is. Otherwise, assign the provider's `capabilities` to the voice in the serialized response. Ensure the merge happens at serialization time only — do not mutate seed data. The output must include `capabilities` on every voice object. Run `npm run test`.

nonGoals: Do NOT introduce integration/e2e test frameworks if not already established
Do NOT change production code beyond what is needed for test assertions
objective: Add or update tests verifying that every provider and voice response includes a well-formed capabilities block, OpenAPI spec validates, and SynthesisJobStatus values round-trip correctly.
planText: 1) Add unit tests asserting that each provider in the catalog has a `capabilities` object with all four required keys (`maxTextLength`, `supportsInstructions`, `supportsStreaming`, `outputFormats`) and correct types. 2) Add unit tests asserting each voice in the catalog has a `capabilities` object, and that voice-level overrides take precedence over provider defaults. 3) Add a test verifying the `SynthesisJobStatus` enum is importable and contains exactly `['pending', 'running', 'completed', 'failed', 'cancelled']`. 4) If an OpenAPI validation script or linter hook exists, add a check that the spec parses without errors. 5) Run `npm run test` — all existing tests must still pass.
